title: cymopterus planosus
intro: Cymopterus planosus, also known as Planos Valley-wild carrot or Planos fern-leaved lomatium, is a perennial herb belonging to the carrot family (Apiaceae) native to the southwestern United States and northwestern Mexico. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly for its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and expectorant properties, which help to combat respiratory issues, wounds, and infections. The health benefits of Cymopterus planosus include relief from colds, coughs, and bronchitis, as well as its potential to reduce fever and alleviate digestive issues. Cymopterus planosus is often prepared as a tincture, infused oil, or dried root for its medicinal uses, although further research is necessary to confirm its efficacy and safety.
